Emily runs a dog rescue center and currently has 25 dogs. On Thursday, she received 4 puppies from a nearby town, bringing the total number of dogs to 29. On Friday, a family brought in a stray dog, increasing the total to 30. On Saturday, 2 families adopted 3 dogs each, reducing the total number of dogs to 24. 

***  

The number of dogs in Emily's rescue center has been affected by several events. Initially, she had 25 dogs, and on Thursday, 4 puppies were added, bringing the total to 29. On Friday, a stray dog was brought in, increasing the count to 30. On Saturday, 2 families adopted 3 dogs each, which reduced the total to 24. The adoption process is a crucial part of the rescue center's operations, as it helps to find homes for dogs in need. 

[address] is a key location where the rescue center operates, and [date] is the specific date when the puppies were received. [name] is the name of the family who brought in the stray dog, and [name] is the name of the family who adopted the dogs on Saturday. [number] is the number of dogs adopted by each family, and [total] is the total number of dogs in the rescue center after the adoption.
